Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Furthrer Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Jul-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Dena L. Narbaitz, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Burlington Coat Factory
Elizabeth Kristen, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Maya Perez

Hearing held. Defendant's motion to compel further responses to discovery:
1. As to Request for Production of Documents No. 25: continued to September 8, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., for further hearing--to be heard with plaintiff's motion to quash subpoenas.
2. As to Special Interrogatories: No. 78 denied; Nos. 79 and 80 granted; Nos. 81 and 82, denied; No. 83 denied without prejudice; Nos. 84 and 85 denied; No. 86 denied without prejudice; No. 87 denied; Nos. 88, 89 granted; Nos. 90 - 94 denied; No. 95 denied without prejudice;
3. As to Request for Production of Documents Nos. 46 and 47 denied without prejudice
4. Request for supplemental information re: privilege log granted; as to those entries addressed in this motion, and not identified as attorney-client or attorney work product, plaintiff shall further amend the privilege log to provide the basis for the claim of privilege, and serve the amend privilege log within fourteen days of this order.
Commissioner: Paul Slavit; Court Reporter: Leslie Tanimura, Telephone # (650) 592-6655

Plaintiff's motion to compel further written discovery.
As to plaintiff's special interrogatories to defendant Burlington, Nos. 9-13, and 15-19, denied without prejudice to propound redrafted interrogatories; as to special interrogatory no. 14, granted
As to plaintiff's special interrogatories to defendant Taylor, Nos. 4-7, denied without prejudice to propound redrafted interrogatories
Any response order shall be verified and served within fourteen days of this order.
